excluded_defs.name: assembleMessage
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand the existing truncation behavior and DATAGRAM_MAX_LENGTH value (65023), used to match the constant in SyslogUdpHandler

exploration_log.aha_moment: UdpSocket::assembleMessage already uses DATAGRAM_MAX_LENGTH = 65023 for truncation. The fix needs to split BEFORE sending so the complete message is delivered across multiple datagrams rather than silently truncated.
exploration_log.hindsight: Reading UdpSocket first was essential to understand the existing truncation and pick the correct max size constant value.

key_decisions.decision: Add chunkLine() method called from write() in SyslogUdpHandler
key_decisions.reasoning: Task says to fix SyslogUdpHandler.php. The handler is the right place to decide how to split messages; UdpSocket should remain a low-level send primitive.

search_sequence.result: Found write() splits by newlines and sends each line via socket.write(). No size check.

search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to understand how write() sends data and what the size limit is
search_sequence.result: Found DATAGRAM_MAX_LENGTH = 65023, assembleMessage truncates to fit

solve_notes: Added `MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD = 65023` constant matching UdpSocket's DATAGRAM_MAX_LENGTH. Added a `chunkLine()` method that splits any line whose byte length exceeds the available payload (after subtracting header length) into smaller chunks with RFC 5424 continuation markers `[N/T]`. Modified `write()` to call `chunkLine()` for each line before passing to `socket->write()`.
task_text:
Fix SyslogUdpHandler not splitting messages exceeding UDP max size

`SyslogUdpHandler` sends the entire formatted message in a single UDP
datagram. Messages exceeding the 65507-byte UDP limit are silently
discarded by the OS. Fix `SyslogUdpHandler.php` to chunk oversized
messages into multiple datagrams with continuation markers per RFC 5424.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: assembleMessage
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: reveals that DATAGRAM_MAX_LENGTH = 65023 is the existing truncation threshold, which must be matched in chunkLine(). Without this, an agent would either guess the max size or use 65507 which could still result in oversized datagrams.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: DATAGRAM_MAX_LENGTH
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the exact value 65023 needs to be known to define MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD correctly in SyslogUdpHandler

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set (write, chunkLine, MAX_UDP_PAYLOAD in SyslogUdpHandler) is what gets edited. The thrash_preventing set adds assembleMessage and DATAGRAM_MAX_LENGTH from UdpSocket because an AI agent implementing chunkLine needs to know the actual size limit value to use — 65023, not the theoretical 65507. Without reading UdpSocket, the agent would likely use 65507 and create an inconsistency with the existing truncation logic.
